checks.evidence: S1 extracted endpoint POST /orders/:id/cancel and 2 permission ACs, but S2 blocked further analysis.

undetermined.hint: S2 flagged AC-2 and AC-3 with 3 blocking_questions; cannot proceed to verdict.
The authorization predicate has two plausible formalizations depending on operator precedence between admin and customer-owner rules.
No AC says what status/body the response must have on success, or what should happen when an order is already in a terminal state (CANCELLED, PAID).
Cannot enumerate boundary behavior for non-pending orders without a declared outcome.
Whether an admin can cancel a PAID order is not addressed.

blocking_questions.question: Can an admin cancel ANY order (including PAID/CANCELLED), or only pending orders? AC-2 says 'admins can cancel orders' and AC-3 restricts customers to their own pending orders; it is unclear whether the PENDING condition also applies to admins.

When an attempt to cancel is not allowed (non-owner, non-admin, or wrong state), what HTTP status and body must the API return? Candidates include 403 Forbidden, 409 Conflict (for already-cancelled), or 404 Not Found to avoid leaking existence.
On successful cancellation, what is the response? (a) 200 with the updated order, (b) 204 No Content, or (c) 202 Accepted if asynchronous? The ticket does not say.
